content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 ,I Street• Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
{916) 322-5660 • Fax (916} 322-0886 
Kevin J. Healy 
Attorney at Law 
2011 Palomar Airport Road, Suite 207 
Carlsbad, CA 92011 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-17-159 
Dear Mr. Healy: 
August 16, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of WLC Architects regarding 
Government Code Section 1090, et seq.1 We are only providing advice under Section 1090, and not 
under other bodies of law governing general conflicts of interest, such as the Political Refonn Act, 
Public Contract Code, or common law. We are also not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In 
re Oglesby ( 1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete 
and accurate. 
We have forwarded your request and all pertinent facts relating to the request to the 
Attorney General's Office and the San Diego County District Attorney's Office. (Section 
1097.l(c)(3).) We did not receive a written response from either entity. (Section 1097.l(c)(4).) And 
the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding against any individual other than 
the requestor." (See Section 1097.l(c)(S).) 
QUESTION 
Does Section I 090 prohibit the City of Carlsbad from entering into a contract for the design 
and construction of Fire Station No. 2 with a proposal team that includes WLC Architects given that 
WLC Architects provided preliminary design work for the future replacement of Fire Station No. ·2 
pursuant to a previous contract with the City? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. Section l 090 prohibits the City from entering into a contract for the design and 
construction of Fire Station No. 2 with a proposal team that includes WLC Architects because WLC 
Architects participated in the making of that contract through its provision of preliminary design 
worf for the future replacement of Fire Station No. 2. 
FACTS 
On September 19, 2016, the City of Carlsbad and WLC Architects ratified a contract for the 
preliminary design of Fire Station No. 2 (the "Preliminary Design Contract"). In a letter from WLC 
1 All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-17-159 
Page No. 2 
Architects to the City dated March 21, 2016 that would later become "Exhibit A" of the Preliminary 
Design Contract, governing the scope of work under that contract, WLC Architects offered to do 
"some preliminary design work for the future replacement of Fire Station No. 2." The Preliminary 
Design Contract anticipated that WLC Architects would provide the City with site plans, floor 
plans, elevations/massing, rendering, and a preliminary cost estimate for Fire Station No. 2, and the 
review of at least two site options for associated below-grade parking. 
The Preliminary Design Contract also required WLC Architects to file a statement of 
economic interests with the City Clerk, reporting investments or interests in all categories, in 
accordance with the requirements of the City's Conflict of Interest Code. WLC Architects charged 
the City a fee of $9,600.00 for its work on the Preliminary Design Contract. 
In November 2016, City voters approved Measure 0, a City ballot measure that authorized 
the City to design and construct Fire Station No. 2. On April 14, 2017, after WLC Architects 
completed its work on the Preliminary Design Contract, the City issued a Request for Statement of 
Qualifications and Proposal ("RSQP") for "Bridging Consultant Services" for Fire Station No. 2 
(the "Bridging Services Contract"). The Bridging Services Contract RSQP incorporates portions of 
WLC Architects' work product under the Preliminary Design Contract. 
The Bridging Services Contract is an intermediate step between the Preliminary Design 
Contract and the Design/Build Contract that would require the selected contractor to review 
documents related to the design and construction of Fire Station No. 2 and to certify in writing that 
Fire Station No. 2 can be designed and built for the amount budgeted. The City's RSQP for the 
Bridging Services Contract notes that the "City reserves the right to not award this project, if it is in 
the City's interests." Addendum No. I to the RSQP for the Bridging Consultant Services Contract 
expressly prohibits WLC Architects from submitting a proposal pursuant to the City's conflict of 
interest policy. However, WLC Architects has confirmed that it will not pursue any work under the 
Bridging Services Contract. 
If the City decides not to pursue the Bridging Services Contract, or after that contract has 
been performed, the City intends to issue a Request for Proposals for the Design and Construction 
Contract (the Design/Build Co.ntract"). WLC Architects would like to be part of a prop.osal team for 
the Design/Build Contract. 
You are the authorized representative ofWLC Architects, and you have asked whether 
Section 1090 applies to WLC Architects with respect to the Design/Build Contract. 
ANALYSIS 
Under Section 1090, "the prohibited act is the making of a contract in which the official has 
a financial interest." (People v. Honig (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 289, 333.) A contract that violates 
Section 1090 is void, and the prohibition applies regardless of whether the terms of the contract are 
fair and equitable to all parties. (Thomson v. Call (1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 646-649.) 
We employ the following six-step analysis to determine whether a public officer or 
employee has a conflict of interest under Section 1090. 


Step One: Is WLC Architects subject to Section 1090? 
File No. A-17-159 
Page No. 3 
Section 1090 provides, in pertinent part, that "[mJembers of the Legislature, state, county, 
district, judicial district, and city officers or employees shall not be financially interested in any 
contract made by them in their official capacity, or by any body or board of which they are 
members." 
Interpreting the term "employees" as used in Section 1090, the California Supreme Court 
recently affirmed the long-standing rule from case law that independent contractors are not 
categorically excluded from Section 1090: "Liability under the statute can extend to independent 
contractors who have duties to engage in or advise on public contracting." (People v. Superior 
Court (Sahlolbei) (2017) 3 Cal.5th 230, 233.) For example, a "corporate consultant" serving as an 
independent contractor for a state or local government agency that "has a hand in designing and 
developing the plans and specifications for the project" is subject to Section l 090. (Davis v. Fresno 
Unified School District (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 261, 300-301.) 
However, not all independent contractors are covered by Section 1090. (Sahlolbei, supra, at 
p. 240.) "'An individual's status as an official under [Section 1090] turns on the extent to which the 
person influences an agency's contracting decisions or otheiwise acts in a capacity that demands the 
public trust.'" (Ibid, quoting Hub City Solid Waste Sen1ices, Inc. v. City of Compton ("Hub City") 
(2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 1114, 1125.) In Hub City, the court held that an independent contractor that 
exerts "considerable influence over the contracting decisions of a public agency" is subject to 
Section 1090. (Hub City, supra, at pp. 1124-1125.) 
Under the Preliminary Design Contract, WLC Architects was charged with delivering site 
plans, floor plans, elevations/massing, rendering, and a preliminary cost estimate for Fire Station 
No. 2, and reviewing at least two site options for associated below-grade parking. Furthermore, the 
Preliminary Design Contract required WLC Architects to file a statement of economic interests with 
the City Clerk, reporting investments or interests in all categories, in accordance with the 
requirements of the City's Conflict oflnterest Code. Finally, the Bridging Services Contract RSQP 
incorporates portions of WLC Architects' preliminary designs for Fire Station No. 2, and the City 
has reserved the ri¥t not to award the Bridging Services Contract if it deterynines doing so would 
not be in its interests. Therefore, WLC Architects is subject to Section 1090 because it had the duty 
"to engage in or advise on public contracting" with respect to the forthcoming Design/Build 
Contract under the Preliminary Design Contract. 
Step Two: Do decisions regarding the design and construction of Fire Station No. 2 involve a 
contract or contracts? 
To determine whether a decision involves a contract, one should refer to general contract 
principles. (See 89 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. ess, 260 (2006); 84 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 34, 36 (2001); 78 
Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 230,234 (1995).) 
WLC Architects has discharged its contractual duties under the Preliminary Design 
Contract. The City has issued the Bridging Services Contract RSQP and may contract with the 
selected independent contractor. The City intends to issue a Request for Proposals for the 
forthcoming Design and Construction Contract, and will enter into the Design/Build Contract with 


File No. A-17-159 
Page No. 4 
the selected proposal team. Therefore, contracts are involved in the decisions regarding the design 
and construction of Fire Station No. 2. 
Step Three: Did WLC Architects make or participate in the making of the forthcoming 
Design/Build Contract? 
Participation in the making of a contract is defined broadly as any act involving preliminary 
discussions, negotiations, compromises, reasoning, planning, drawing of plans and specifications, 
and solicitation for bids. (Millbrae Assn. for Residential Survival v. City of Millbrae (1968) 262 
Cal.App.2d 222,237; see also Stigall v. City of Taft (1962) 48 Cal.2d 565, 569.) 
Pursuant to the Preliminary Design Contract, WLC Architects was charged with delivering 
site plans, floor plans, elevations/massing, rendering, and a preliminary cost estimate for Fire 
Station No. 2, and the review of at least two site options for associated below-grade parking. The 
City incorporated portions of WLC Architects' work product under the Preliminary Design 
Contract into its Bridging Services Contract RSQP. And the Bridging Services Contract RSQP 
expressly prohibited WLC Architects from submitting a proposal for that contract. These facts 
indicate that WLC Architects has had considerable influence over the design of Fire Station No. 2. 
The City's RSQP for the Bridging Services Contract provides that the City may not award 
the Bridging Services Contract if it detennines it is not in its interest do so. If the City awards the 
Bridging Services Contract, the selected contractor will be charged with reviewing documents 
related to the design and construction of Fire Station No. 2, including WLC Architect's preliminary 
design work and cost estimate for Fire Station No. 2, and certifying in writing that Fire Station No. 
2 can be designed and built for the amount budgeted. Therefore, the ultimate contract to construct 
Fire Station No. 2 will be based at least in part on WLC Architects' preliminary design work, 
regardless of whether the City awards the Bridging Services Contract. Accordingly, we find that 
WLC Architects participated in the making of the forthcoming Design/Build Contract for Fire 
Station No. 2 through its perfonnance of its duties under the Preliminary Design Contract. 
Step Four: Docs WLC Architects have a financial interest in the forthcoming Design/Build 
Contract? 
For Section I 090 to apply, the public official in question must have a financial interest in the 
contract at issue. Although Section I 090 does not specifically define "financial interest," an 
examination of case law and statutory exceptions to the basic prohibition indicate that the tenn is to 
be liberally interpreted. (See People v. Deysher (1934) 2 Cal.2d 141, 146.) "[T]he certainty of 
financial gain is not necessary to create a conflict of interest .... The government's right to the 
absolute, undivided allegiance of a public officer is diminished as effectively where the officer acts 
with a hope of personal financial gain as where he acts with certainty." (People v. Gnass (2002) 101 
Cal.App.4th 1271, 1298 (citations omitted).) 
I 
WLC Architects is financially interested in the forthcoming Design/Build Contract for Fire 
Station No. 2 because WLC Architects will secure additional business, and thus additional profit, 
from that contract if it is part of the proposal team selected by the City for that project. 


File No. A-17-159 
Page No. 5 
Steps Five and Six: Docs a remote or noninterest exception or the rule of necessity apply? 
Because there is no indication from the facts presented that a remote or noninterest 
exception or the rule of necessity applies, Section I 090 prohibits the City from entering into the 
Design/Build Contract for Fire Station No. 2 with a proposal team that includes WLC Architects. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
MFC:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Jack C. Woodside 
General Counsel 
tf4{1 
Matthew F. Christy 
Counsel, Legal Division

worf for the future replacement of Fire Station No. 2.

and construction of Fire Station No. 2.

Participation in the making of a contract is defined broadly as any act involving preliminary

WLC Architects is financially interested in the forthcoming Design/Build Contract for Fire
